title: src_eui64 logged via LOG_DBG in lichen_l2_input()
description:
In lichen_l2.c:501-504, the full 8-byte source EUI-64 from received frames is logged via LOG_DBG:
LOG_DBG("Decompressed %zu bytes from %02x:%02x:%02x:%02x:%02x:%02x:%02x:%02x", ...)

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:501-504

While LOG_DBG is typically compiled out in production, if debug logging is enabled, every received frame's sender identity is logged. This creates a log-based traffic analysis vector.

Impact: When debug logging is enabled, device identities of all communicating peers are logged. Lower priority since LOG_DBG is off by default.

Recommendation: Consider truncating to last 4 bytes or using a hash for debugging purposes while preserving debuggability.
close_reason: Fixed: Added SECURITY comment explaining why logging EUI-64 at DEBUG level is acceptable (requires explicit enablement, authenticated peers only, exposed in L2/IPv6 addresses anyway, needed for mesh debugging)
